_metadata.source: Clinical pharmacology literature and FDA prescribing information

potassium_high.comment: Drug-induced hyperkalemia risk. Monitor K+ closely. Consider alternative or dose reduction.

potassium_low.comment: Drug-induced hypokalemia. Monitor K+. Consider K+ supplementation or K+-sparing agent.

inr_high.comment: Patient on warfarin. Consider dose reduction or withholding. Check for interacting drugs.

inr_high_interactions.comment: These drugs increase warfarin effect (CYP2C9/3A4 inhibition). INR monitoring required if co-prescribed with warfarin.
inr_high_interactions.source: Holbrook et al. Chest 2012 (Systematic review of warfarin interactions)

creatinine_rising.comment: Rising creatinine in context of nephrotoxic drug. Assess for drug-induced AKI. Consider dose adjustment or alternative.

liver_enzymes_elevated.comment: Elevated transaminases may be drug-induced hepatotoxicity. Check timeline against drug initiation. Consider discontinuation if ALT >3x ULN with symptoms or >5x ULN asymptomatic.
liver_enzymes_elevated.source: ACG Drug-Induced Liver Injury 2014; Chalasani et al. Am J Gastroenterol

glucose_low.comment: Hypoglycemia from insulin or sulfonylurea. Review dose. Elderly and renal impairment increase risk.

thyroid_abnormal.comment: Thyroid dysfunction may be drug-induced. Amiodarone: both hyper and hypothyroidism. Lithium: hypothyroidism. Checkpoint inhibitors: immune thyroiditis.

platelet_low.comment: Thrombocytopenia may be drug-induced. Check heparin (HIT risk), cytotoxic agents, antibiotics. Consider urgent haematology review if <50.

magnesium_low.comment: Drug-induced hypomagnesemia. PPI-associated (>3 months use). Diuretic-associated. Monitor and replace.
magnesium_low.source: FDA Safety Communication 2011 (PPIs); Hess et al. Eur J Intern Med 2022
